description: You are a 5th-grade science teacher at an OLPC-equipped school preparing a demonstration of how programming can create scientific visualizations. Write a Python program in the Sugar Pippy activity that generates an SVG diagram of the solar system and saves it to /home/ga/Documents/solar_system.svg. The SVG must include the Sun as a large yellow, orange, or gold circle, and all 8 planets (Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune) as circles or ellipses with distinct colors and text labels. Ensure the planets have varying radii reflecting their approximate relative sizes (e.g., Jupiter as the largest and Mercury as the smallest). Save the Pippy program to the Sugar Journal with the title 'Solar System Generator'.
